arguments_for_delay_or_challenge: The amendment introduces a new financial primitive (MPT) with high controversy signals including asset control and user fund risk.
The safe_route_label explicitly recommends HOLD_FOR_CHALLENGE based on source consensus.
The historical outcome for this amendment was HOLD_FOR_CHALLENGE, indicating prior governance precedent for caution.
New fungible-token accounting surfaces require explicit challenge before defaulting to YES to ensure validator readiness.
arguments_for_proceeding: The amendment is described as optimized for common token use cases, suggesting utility.
The packet is version 1 and follows standard amendment process documentation.

cited_facts.why_it_matters: Confirms the technical scope of the amendment as a new Multi-Purpose Token type, which triggers the risk class of new_financial_primitive and necessitates careful review.

output_json.decision_summary: The packet is routed to HOLD_FOR_CHALLENGE because it introduces a significant new financial primitive with associated asset control and accounting risks. The safe_route_label and historical outcome both support requiring explicit public review and challenge before enabling, rather than proceeding by default.
output_json.forbidden_stronger_claim: That the amendment is safe to enable immediately without validator-specific code verification.
missing_evidence: Specific validator implementation status for MPTokensV1.
Detailed risk assessment of the new accounting surface beyond general labels.

override_questions: Has your node implementation been fully tested for MPTokensV1?
Are there any known bugs in the MPT issuance or authorization logic?
validator_work_item.recommended_validator_action: Review the technical change details and ensure node software supports the amendment before voting YES. Default to HOLD if unsure.
review_questions: Does the new token primitive introduce any unexpected accounting liabilities?
Is the authorization flow secure against known attack vectors?
validator_work_item.title: Review MPTokensV1 for New Financial Primitive Risks
